description.en: Sacred cultural/religious plant of Sri Lanka; the scientific binomial name **Nelumbo nucifera comes directly from the Sinhala word *nelum/nelambu***. Marginal aquatic perennial — grows from thickened rhizomes rooted in the mud of ponds, rice-fallow paddy lowlands, and tanks. Leaves circular and peltate (distinguishing from Nymphaea water-lilies). **Lotus root is a popular Sri Lankan vegetable cooked in coconut-milk curries.** Seeds and flowers are also used. Grows best in organically rich loam under calm water up to 6 ft deep in full sun.

scientific_name.quote: Nelumbo nucifera comes from a Sinhalese name for the plant, nelambu or nelum

description.quote: The lotus species occurs in Sri Lanka, and this distribution is probably the result of human translocations. Lotus root is a popular vegetable in Sri Lanka, where it is often cooked in coconut milk gravy.
